title: Amphetamine: MedlinePlus Drug Information

Tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking the following medications or have stopped taking them within the past two weeks: isocarboxazid (Marplan Â® ), linezolid (Zyvox Â® ), methylene blue, phenelzine (Nardil Â® ), selegiline (Eldepryl Â® , Emsam Â® , Zelapar Â® ), and tranylcypromine (Parnate Â® ). Some medications should not be taken with amphetamine. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with amphetamine. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting amphetamine with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking amphetamine, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. 
. The following nonprescription or herbal products may interact with amphetamine: St. John's wort; tryptophan or nutritional supplements you are taking including glutamic acid (L-glutamine); sodium acid phosphate; sodium bicarbonate (Arm and Hammer â¢ Baking Soda, Soda Mint); medications for heartburn or ulcers such as cimetidine (Tagamet Â® ), esomeprazole (Nexium Â® ), omeprazole (Prilosec Â® , in Zegerid Â® ); allergy medications (diphenhydramine, loratadine, fexofenadine, chlorpheniramine or cetirizine). Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking amphetamin
